name: Tres Pacos Mine and Cuevas de Almanzora Reservoir Loop
name_original: Minas de los Tres Pacos — circular por el Embalse de Cuevas de Almanzora
description: This difficult 30.4-mile mountain biking route circles the Embalse de Cuevas de Almanzora, exploring the historic Minas de los Tres Pacos.
region_intro: Mountain biking around Huércal-Overa offers diverse terrain, from fertile plains along watercourses to arid, scrub-covered hills. The region features varied challenges and scenic backdrops for cycling, with geological formations like gypsum and limestone visible along many routes. The landscape transitions from river plains to higher elevations, providing a range of gradients for mountain bike trails. Best mountain bike trails around Huércal-Overa The most popular mountain bike trail is Tres Pacos Mine and Cuevas de Almanzora Reservoir Loop, a 30.4 miles (49.0 km) trail that takes 4 hours 33 minutes to complete. This difficult route explores historic mines and circles the Cuevas de Almanzora Reservoir. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Almendricos Greenway – Solar Panels at Los Naranjos loop from Huércal-Overa, a moderate 23.1 miles (37.2 km) path. This trail showcases the "Green Way," an old railway line repurposed for cycling. Local mountain bikers also love the Collado de la Rellena loop from Huércal-Overa, a 18.9 miles (30.4 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 2 hours 32 minutes. Mountain biking around Huércal-Overa is defined by fertile plains, arid hills, and geological formations. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy routes to challenging paths. The routes in Huércal-Overa are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 20 reviews. More than 200 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Huércal-Overa's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Huércal-Overa?
A: There are over 50 mountain bike trails around Huércal-Overa, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels and preferences. The komoot community has mapped 57 routes in total.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners or families with children?
A: Yes, Huércal-Overa offers trails suitable for beginners and families. While there is one specifically easy route, many of the 48 moderate trails are also accessible. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38224090" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almendricos Greenway – Solar Panels at Los Naranjos loop from Huércal-Overa&lt;/a&gt;, for example, follows an old railway line (Vía Verde) with relatively flat terrain, making it a good option for a wider range of riders.

Q: What kind of challenges can I expect on advanced mountain bike trails in Huércal-Overa?
A: For experienced riders, there are 8 difficult mountain bike trails that offer significant challenges. These routes often feature substantial elevation gains, rocky terrain, and fast-paced singletrack sections. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1017846179/tres-pacos-mine-and-cuevas-de-almanzora-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tres Pacos Mine and Cuevas de Almanzora Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a prime example, covering 49 km with over 1,100m of elevation gain.

Q: When is the best time of year for mountain biking in Huércal-Overa?
A: The best time for mountain biking in Huércal-Overa is typically during the spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November) months. During these periods, the weather is generally milder and more pleasant, avoiding the intense heat of summer and potential winter rains. This allows for more comfortable and enjoyable rides across the region's diverse terrain.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in the area?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike trails around Huércal-Overa are circular, allowing you to start and end your ride in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31546694" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Collado de la Rellena loop from Huércal-Overa&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38254894" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cuevas de Almanzora Reservoir loop from Repsol&lt;/a&gt;, which offer varied distances and elevations.

Q: What interesting natural features or historical sites can I see along the mountain bike trails?
A: The trails around Huércal-Overa offer a rich blend of natural beauty and historical intrigue. You can encounter diverse geological formations like gypsum and limestone, and explore areas like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5578174" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cuevas del Calguerín&lt;/a&gt;. Historically, you might pass by sites such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2625021" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tres Pacos Mine&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2610833" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruins of Huércal la Vieja Fortress&lt;/a&gt;, offering glimpses into the region's past.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about the trails in Huércal-Overa?
A: The mountain bike trails in Huércal-Overa are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 20 reviews. Riders often praise the diverse terrain, which transitions from fertile plains to arid hills, and the scenic backdrops. The variety of routes, from easy to challenging, is also frequently highlighted as a positive aspect.

Q: Are there specific rules or considerations for mountain biking with dogs in Huércal-Overa?
A: While the region offers many outdoor opportunities, specific rules for mountain biking with dogs can vary, especially in natural parks or protected areas. It's always advisable to keep dogs on a leash, especially when encountering wildlife or other trail users, and to ensure they are well-behaved and do not disturb the natural environment. Always check local regulations for any specific trail you plan to ride.

Q: Where can I find parking for mountain bike trails in Huércal-Overa?
A: Many trailheads and starting points for mountain bike routes in Huércal-Overa offer designated parking areas. For routes that start directly from the town, you can often find public parking. For trails further afield, such as those near the Cuevas de Almanzora Reservoir, specific parking spots are usually indicated on komoot route maps or local signage. It's recommended to check the route details on komoot for specific parking information before you go.

Q: Are there any permits required for mountain biking in Huércal-Overa, especially in natural areas?
A: Generally, permits are not required for mountain biking on most trails in the Huércal-Overa region. However, if you plan to ride within specific natural parks or protected areas, it's always a good practice to check with local authorities or park management for any specific regulations or permit requirements that might apply. The Sierra Espuña Natural Park, for instance, is a nearby area where such rules might be in place.

Q: What is the average length and duration of mountain bike routes in Huércal-Overa?
A: The mountain bike routes in Huércal-Overa vary significantly in length and duration. You can find shorter rides, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31546694" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Collado de la Rellena loop&lt;/a&gt; at 30.4 km (approx. 2.5 hours), to longer, more challenging expeditions such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1017846179/tres-pacos-mine-and-cuevas-de-almanzora-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tres Pacos Mine and Cuevas de Almanzora Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which is 49 km and can take over 4.5 hours. There are options to suit different time commitments and fitness levels.

Q: Are there places to eat or stay near the mountain bike trails in Huércal-Overa?
A: Huércal-Overa and its surrounding areas offer various options for accommodation and dining. You'll find cafes, restaurants, and pubs within the town itself, and some smaller establishments might be located in villages closer to specific trailheads. For accommodation, there are hotels and guesthouses available. It's advisable to check local listings for options closest to your chosen starting point.
tips: Swamp located in the province of Almería, on the waters of the Almanzora river. The reservoir was built in 1986 to supply the nearby populations, for irrigation and for the production of electricity.

The Tres Pacos mine is an abandoned mine located in the municipality of Cuevas de Almanzora. Iron minerals such as hematite were extracted from this mine.

The mine began operating at the end of the 19th century and until 1935, when the activity was abandoned. The mineral was transported by an aerial cable to the Cala Picota pier, 13 kilometers away, completely crossing the Almagro mountain range. The mineral was first processed in the mine itself, using various furnaces fueled by charcoal.

Excellent viewpoint with views of the reservoir fed by the waters of the Almanzora River, which descends from Sierra de Los Filabres. It was created for irrigation and supply, although in winter it is also usually a place for fishing.

The Sendero Huércal La Vieja (PR-A 323) runs along the eastern end of the Sierra de Almagro. It is in the Levante Almeriense region at the crossroads of the Mediterranean corridor, and all the gorges and ravines in the municipality flow into the Almanzora river basin.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of the Minas de los Tres Pacos — circular por el Embalse de Cuevas de Almanzora trail?
faqs.answer: This mountain biking route is rated as difficult. With a distance of nearly 49 km and over 1,100 meters of elevation gain, it requires good fitness and experience with varied terrain. While some sections around the reservoir might be easier, the overall circuit presents a significant challenge.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this mountain biking route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied, reflecting the region's natural and industrial history. You'll encounter rugged, industrially influenced landscapes around the Tres Pacos Mine, with remnants of old mining operations. The route also circles the Cuevas de Almanzora Reservoir, offering scenic views along natural areas characterized by bushy, herbaceous vegetation and craggy mountains. Expect gravel surfaces and some concrete paths like the Camino de Los Albarracines.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: The trail is rich in both natural beauty and industrial heritage. Key highlights include the historic Tres Pacos Mine, where you can see remains of old calcination ovens and treatment plants. You'll also enjoy stunning views of the Cuevas de Almanzora Reservoir, particularly from the Viewpoint of Cuevas de Almanzora Reservoir. The route also passes near the Ruins of Huércal la Vieja Fortress.

faqs.question: Is the Minas de los Tres Pacos — circular por el Embalse de Cuevas de Almanzora trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Generally, dogs are allowed on trails in this region, but it's crucial to keep them on a leash, especially given the presence of wildlife like the spur-thighed tortoise and various bird species. The trail's length and difficulty, combined with the arid climate, mean you should ensure your dog is fit enough and carry plenty of water for both of you.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this trail?
faqs.answer: Given the Mediterranean and arid climate of Almería, the best times to tackle this trail are during the spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November). Temperatures are milder, making the long distance and elevation gain more manageable. Summers can be extremely hot, and winters might bring cooler, but generally still pleasant, conditions.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trail?
faqs.answer: While specific parking areas for this exact route are not detailed, the trail is circular around the Cuevas de Almanzora Reservoir. You can typically find parking in or near the town of Cuevas de Almanzora, or at various access points around the reservoir itself. It's advisable to check local maps for designated parking spots closest to your preferred starting point.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this mountain biking route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to mountain bike the Minas de los Tres Pacos — circular por el Embalse de Cuevas de Almanzora trail. It is generally open to the public for recreational use.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of several other paths. You will find yourself on parts of Los reforestadores de la Sierra de Almagro, Huércal La Vieja, and the Camino de los Albarracines, among others. These intersections can offer alternative exploration opportunities if you wish to extend or modify your ride.

faqs.question: What wildlife might I encounter along the reservoir?
faqs.answer: The natural areas around the Cuevas de Almanzora Reservoir are home to various species. Keep an eye out for the spur-thighed tortoise and a diverse range of bird species that inhabit the water and surrounding bushy vegetation.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a mountain bike ride on this trail?
faqs.answer: Given the trail's difficulty, length, and arid climate, essential items include plenty of water (more than you think you'll need), snacks, sun protection (sunscreen, hat, sunglasses), a repair kit for your bike, a first-aid kit, and a fully charged phone with the Komoot app for navigation. Appropriate mountain biking attire and a helmet are, of course, mandatory.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or restaurants near the trail?
faqs.answer: While the trail itself is primarily natural and industrial landscape, the nearby town of Cuevas de Almanzora offers various amenities, including cafes, bars, and restaurants where you can refuel before or after your ride. It's a good idea to plan your stops, as facilities are not directly on the route.
